title: Open To All, Tomorrow

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A Sunday balanced on one question — would the Iran war actually end tomorrow, as Trump posted and Pakistan's PM hinted, or was nothing finalised, as Tehran insisted and Israel's cabinet feared — while steady markets, a coronavirus-vaccine breakthrough and a France-sized marine reserve nudged the day faintly upward against a Mindanao quake aftermath, Sudan's famine and another night of strikes on Ukraine.

style.genre: dub / neo-soul — sampled electric-piano chords over a spacious TR-808 with tape-echo delays, plucked guitar topline

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano (filtered into a soft Rhodes-ish electric piano) for the chords, acoustic guitar (VCSL) for the dubbed topline, RolandTR808 drum machine with tape-echo space; supporting sine sub-bass and a low sawtooth pad
pattern: stack(s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~, ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh, ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.46).hpf(40).room(0.22).rsize(5).delay(0.18).delaytime(0.5), note("d1 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ a1 ~ ~ ~ d1 ~ ~ ~ c2 ~").s("sine").attack(0.01).release(0.5).lpf(360).gain(0.52), note("<[d3,fs3,a3,c4] [g2,b2,d3,fs3] [a2,cs3,e3,g3] [c3,e3,g3,a3]>").s("piano").lpf(sine.range(560,1450).slow(16)).gain(0.3).attack(0.02).release(0.7).room(0.36).rsize(6).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.375).pan(0.45).slow(2), note("<[d4,a4,d5] [d4,b4,d5] [cs4,a4,e5] [c4,g4,e5]>").s("sawtooth").attack(3.5).decay(1.5).sustain(0.4).release(5).lpf(sine.range(440,1000).slow(28)).gain(0.06).room(0.6).rsize(8), note("a4 ~ d5 ~ ~ c5 ~ b4 ~ ~ a4 ~ fs4 ~ ~ ~ ~ g4 ~ a4 ~ ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ c5 ~ b4 ~ a4 ~").s("guitar").gain(0.32).lpf(2600).room(0.42).rsize(6).pan(0.6).delay(0.33).delaytime(0.5).off(0.25, x => x.gain(0.12).pan(0.72).lpf(1800)).gain(sine.range(0.26,0.38).slow(18)))

lines: He posts it in capitals — signed by tomorrow, the strait swinging open to all,
no money changes hands and the war just dissolves, planes pre-loading for the Geneva call;
but Tehran says nothing's been settled at all, and the cabinet meets behind a closed door,
so the calm isn't peace, it's a held kind of quiet, an uneasy sky over an unsigned war.
Mindanao still counts in the dust of the landslide, El Fasher goes hungry another long week,
the drones found a market in the dark over Dnipro, and the floor of the day stays low where it leaks;
but the markets read relief and closed a little higher, a vaccine cleared its first human trial clean,
a reserve the size of France around the islands — the bright keeps arriving in between.
Open to all, tomorrow — the word he keeps saying, the date that the other side won't sign,
so I carry the maybe on a slow dub echo, the hope and the doubt running line for line;
an uneasy calm, not a sigh and not a siren, the quake and the cure on the same gray tide —
I'll take tomorrow if tomorrow keeps its promise, and hold the breath steady till the ink is dried.
